title: The Complete Seq2Seq Flow
concept: A complete translation pipeline: load text, tokenize to subwords, embed, encode with Bi-LSTM, align with attention, decode with beam search.

exercises.prompt: What does the abbreviation 'Seq2Seq' stand for?
exercises.explain: Seq2Seq stands for Sequence-to-Sequence, which converts one sequence (like a sentence in English) into another (like a sentence in French).

cards.title: The Translation Factory
cards.body: Have you ever wondered how Google Translate instantly converts entire paragraphs into another language? It is not just a simple dictionary lookup; it is a highly coordinated pipeline of neural networks working in harmony!

cards.title: The Complete Seq2Seq Flow
cards.body: The Seq2Seq pipeline is a multi-step factory line. It starts with raw text, breaks it into subwords, converts them into numeric embeddings, processes them through a Bi-LSTM encoder, uses attention to focus on key words, and finally uses a decoder with beam search to generate the translation.

cards.title: The Shipping Port Analogy
cards.body: Think of Seq2Seq like an international shipping port. Raw goods (text) are packed into standardized containers (tokens), loaded onto cargo ships (encoder), guided through busy canals by tugboats (attention), and finally unloaded and sorted into a new country's delivery trucks (decoder).

cards.title: Seq2Seq in Action
cards.body:
When translating 'I love coding' to Spanish:
1. Tokenize: ['I', ' love', ' cod', 'ing']
2. Embed: Convert tokens to vectors.
3. Encode: Bi-LSTM reads forward and backward.
4. Align: Attention links English words to Spanish equivalents.
5. Decode: Beam search predicts 'Me encanta programar' step-by-step.

cards.title: Myth: Word-by-Word Translation
cards.body: People often think translation models translate word-by-word in a straight line. In reality, modern Seq2Seq systems look at the entire sentence at once, using attention to jump back and forth and translate naturally.

cards.title: Input vs. Output Flow
cards.body: A comparison between the input and output stages of a complete sequence-to-sequence translation pipeline.

left.text: Raw text is broken into subwords and converted into dense vector embeddings.

right.text: The decoder uses beam search to generate the best final translated text.

exercises.prompt: What is the main purpose of using Beam Search in the decoder phase?

options.text: To keep track of multiple translation paths and choose the most likely overall sentence.
To read the source sentence backward.
To force the model to look at only one word at a time.

exercises.explain: Beam search keeps track of multiple high-probability translation paths simultaneously to choose the best final sentence.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Subword tokenization helps the pipeline handle words it has never seen before by breaking them into smaller parts.
exercises.explain: Subword tokenization splits rare or complex words into smaller, manageable pieces (like 'play' and 'ing') so the model never runs into unknown words.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing component that links the encoder and decoder.
exercises.sentence: The encoder processes the input text, and the attention mechanism aligns it with the decoder's targets.
exercises.explain: The attention mechanism calculates weight scores to align matching words between the input and output languages.

exercises.prompt: Which translation system does NOT use a modern Seq2Seq pipeline?

options.text: System A: Converts the entire paragraph to tokens, encodes context with a Bi-LSTM, and generates natural flowing sentences.
System B: Replaces every English word with the exact corresponding word in a Spanish dictionary file.
System C: Analyzes the sentence structure using attention layers to figure out the best word order for translation.

exercises.explain: AI models use complex pipelines involving tokenization, encoding, attention, and decoding. System B is a simple, inflexible dictionary lookup.

exercises.prompt: How do the core components of a Seq2Seq pipeline work together to translate a sentence?

options.text: The encoder directly creates the output tokens without a decoder.
The attention mechanism bypasses the encoder entirely.
The encoder processes the input, attention links the states, and the decoder outputs the target.
Subword tokenization is only used at the very end of the pipeline.

exercises.explain: In a complete Seq2Seq system, the encoder reads the input first, attention dynamically connects the encoder and decoder states, and the decoder generates the final translation.
